summary: A hissy soundboard capture of a shaky high‑school set with occasional bright moments – Lovelight and Good Morning Little Schoolgirl shine despite overall uneven performance.
review: The recording is a typical 1969 gym tape – clear enough to hear the band but plagued by hiss and poor acoustics. Musically the show is ragged, with sloppy playing early on and a short set curtailed by curfew; however Bob Weir rescues several numbers and Lovelight plus Good Morning Little Schoolgirl provide memorable highlights. Overall it’s an interesting historical document more than a recommended listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: 19 reviews give an average of ~2.8 stars; most note poor sound and uneven playing, but a few highlight bright moments, leading to a mixed overall assessment.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Many reviewers call the tape "hissy" and complain about gym acoustics; a few say the sound is decent enough for a quick download, but overall clarity is limited.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Short (≈50‑60 min) set cut short by a 11 pm curfew; the first half feels ragged and unfocused, but Bob Weir’s vocals/guitar lift the second half and the Lovelight encore adds a pleasant finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Often described as out of sync and uncommitted (no smoking allowed, sloppiness), though a few notes mention occasional nice guitar lines.
band_member_comments.Bob: Consistently praised for pulling the show together – strong vocals and creative rhythm‑guitar work that "saved" the performance.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart’s timing is criticized on some tracks, but a couple of interesting grooves are noted.
